text: The surgeon begins the procedure by performing sharp dissection of adhesions, revealing a thickened peritoneal reflection indicative of chronic disease.
The surgeon begins the procedure by performing sharp dissection of adhesions, revealing a thickened peritoneal reflection indicative of chronic disease.

The surgeon dissects the posterior aspect of the rectum, continuing laterally. The anterior peritoneal reflection is opened, and dissection proceeds through the pelvis until the levator muscles are visible.

The surgeon dissects the posterior aspect of the rectum, progressing laterally. The anterior peritoneal reflection is opened, and the dissection continues through the pelvis until the levator muscles are visible.
A rectangular-shaped mesh is placed on the anterior surface of the rectum and is secured to the ventral wall using separate sutures.
A rectangular-shaped mesh is positioned on the anterior surface of the rectum and secured to the ventral wall using separate sutures.
The surgeon secures the mesh to the promontorium using spiral tackers, then closes the peritoneum to prevent direct contact between intra-abdominal organs and the mesh.
The surgeon fixes the mesh onto the promontorium using spiral tackers. Subsequently, the peritoneum is closed to prevent direct contact between intra-abdominal organs and the mesh.
